rag_rules: Preserve the existing AADL structure and insert new contract content only within the owning component or implementation scope.
Keep type-level behavioral contracts separate from implementation-level content; do not move architecture elements or alter non-annex structure.
generic_rules: Match inserted content to the correct owner scope so references remain valid for that owner.
Preserve existing subcomponents, connections, features, properties, and unrelated annex content unchanged.
Avoid introducing references that depend on names or paths not visible from the selected owner scope.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE content is enclosed in an `annex agree {** ... **};` block.
Component type scopes are the normal location for assumptions and guarantees; implementation scopes are typically for implementation-level bindings when valid there.
anti_patterns: Do not place type-scope contract content into an implementation owner just because that implementation is the current edit target.
Do not rewrite architecture structure, relocate existing declarations, or add subcomponent-path references that are invalid in the chosen owner scope.
irrelevant_topics: Temporal recurrence and branching expression patterns
Scalar helper type details and numeric comparison rules unless editing existing AGREE declarations requires them
